sanskrit: दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः। उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः॥
transliteration: doṣair etaiḥ kula-ghnānāṃ varṇa-saṅkara-kārakaiḥ | utsādyante jāti-dharmāḥ kula-dharmāś ca śāśvatāḥ ||
literal_meaning: By these faults of the family-slayers, the makers of caste-confusion, the eternal duties of class and family are uprooted.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna projects catastrophic collapse onto the future: if he kills his relatives, society’s moral scaffolding will disintegrate, revealing his terror that personal action can irreversibly fracture communal identity. The verse voices the anxious fantasy that one’s choices are never private—they ripple outward, erasing timeless structures that give life coherence.
psychological_state: A person frozen by anticipatory guilt, convinced that any decisive act will unleash irreversible cultural and moral devastation.
trigger_scenario: A first-generation college student contemplating a career path their immigrant family sees as betrayal, fearing it will ‘break the family line’ and dissolve cherished traditions.
anchor_text: Your choices echo beyond you; uphold duty to protect the world’s moral fabric.
